article_title: Obexelimab Cuts IgG4-Related Disease Flares by 56% in Phase 3 INDIGO Trial, Published in NEJM
summary: Fully-verified Phase 3 INDIGO coverage; every clinical statistic confirmed against the cited press releases. The lone automated warning (NEJM 403) is non-blocking — NEJM is cited only for the DOI/publication fact, which is corroborated by GlobeNewswire.

findings.message: Bot-blocked source (NEJM, HTTP 403) cited only for the publication fact — no claim rests on it alone.
findings.details: https://www.nejm.org/doi/abs/10.1056/NEJMoa2601337 is cited solely for 'published in NEJM' and the DOI (10.1056/NEJMoa2601337). Both are independently confirmed by the GlobeNewswire release ('simultaneously presented at the EULAR 2026 Congress and published in the New England Journal of Medicine'). Every numeric endpoint is anchored to a non-blocked press release. No corrective action required.

content_preview.title: Obexelimab Cuts IgG4-Related Disease Flares by 56% in Phase 3 INDIGO Trial, Published in NEJM
content_preview.summary: Zenas BioPharma's obexelimab met its primary endpoint and all four key secondary endpoints in the Phase 3 INDIGO trial, with results published in NEJM and presented at EULAR 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Zenas BioPharma reported on [June 2, 2026](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/06/02/3305741/0/en/zenas-biopharma-announces-data-from-the-registrational-phase-3-indigo-trial-of-obexelimab-in-immunoglobulin-g4-related-disease-igg4-rd-simultaneously-presented-at-the-eular-2026-co.html) that its investigational antibody obexelimab produced a 56% reduction in the risk of disease flare compared with placebo in the registrational Phase 3 INDIGO trial of patients with immunoglo...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent, disciplined clinical News piece (627 words) on the Phase 3 INDIGO readout — every endpoint attributed to a specific press release, with the NEJM publication cited only for the publication fact and the full safety/durability caveats clearly stated.
editor_notes.source_verification: Four of five snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (GlobeNewswire) confirms the 56% flare-risk reduction, 194 patients, 250 mg subcutaneous weekly for 52 weeks, the bifunctional CD19/FcγRIIb mechanism, 37.1% vs 19.6% complete remission, the 329.5 mg vs 929.8 mg glucocorticoid figures, the EULAR June 4 London presentation, and both quotes (Lisa von Moltke's 'largest clinical trial ever conducted in IgG4-RD' and Emanuel Della Torre's 'novel, highly active, self-administered therapy', with his Vita-Salute San Raffaele affiliation). source-1 (StockTitan) confirms HR 0.44 (95% CI 0.277-0.711; p=0.0005), 73.2% vs 45.4% flare-free, grade ≥3 TEAEs 11.3% vs 23.7%, SAEs 10.3% vs 18.6%, the May 2026 BLA, and the 'B cell lineage' detail. source-2 (BioSpace) confirms the secondary-endpoint statistics (HR 0.41, 95% CI 0.26-0.66, p=0.0001, 59%; HR 0.48, 95% CI 0.32-0.74, p=0.0008, 52%; p=0.0049; the 65% steroid reduction). source-4 (NCBI StatPearls) confirms the disease background (pancreas/retroperitoneum organ involvement, corticosteroid mainstay, 'Relapse rates are between 10% to 53%'). source-3 (NEJM) returned HTTP 403 (bot-blocked).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every statistic — relative reductions, hazard ratios, confidence intervals, p-values, remission rates, glucocorticoid totals, and safety percentages — is verbatim in the specific outlet it is cited to, with disciplined single-outlet attribution and no compound citations. The NEJM 403 is correctly handled per Rule 9: NEJM is cited only for the DOI/publication fact, which is corroborated by GlobeNewswire; no numeric claim depends on the blocked source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and precise; 'What We Don't Know' appropriately flags the topline/conference nature of the data, the absent FDA action date, and the lack of long-term organ-fibrosis outcomes.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior obexelimab / IgG4-RD / Zenas coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified medical submission with exemplary statistic attribution and correct handling of the bot-blocked primary publication. APPROVE.
